Q35 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements :
(a) Every conflict serializable schedule is also 2PL.
(b) Strict 2PL is not free from starvation.
(c) For queries of the form hashing is usually better than a B+ tree.
(d) For queries of the form hashing is usually better than a B+ tree.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Every 2PL is conflict serializable schedule but not vice-versa. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Strict 2PL is not free from starvation. So, option (b) is correct.
• A secondary index has to be dense since the file is not organized according to a secondary index. Otherwise the index structure does not allow to find all values directly.

Q36 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements :
(a) Every DCFL is unambiguous but every unambiguous is not DCFL.
(b) Language of grammar parsed by LR(2) parsers is a strict super set of the languages of grammars parsed by LR(1) parsers.
(c) A LR(0) parser can parse any regular grammar.
(d) A LR(0) configuration set cannot have both shift as well as reduce items.
Which of the above is/are true?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• LR(0) CLR (1) = LR(2) ... LR(K) = LR(K + 1); for K >= 1.
LR(K) = LR(K + 1) so, language of grammars parsed by LR(2) parsers is not a strict superset of the languages of grammar parsed by LR(1) parsers although they both are same. If you compare their grammar then LR(0) CLR(1) CLR(2) ... LR(K) CLR(K + 1). So, option (b) is false.
• No grammar with empty production can be LR(0). But empty rules allowed in regular grammar as the only condition for a grammar to be regular is to be left or right linear.

Q42 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements :
(a) Privileged instruction cannot be executed in Kernel mode.
(b) If a Kernel is single threaded, system call from any thread can block the entire task.
(c) Demand paging requires more main memory than normal paging.
(d) Continuous file allocation leads to external fragmentation.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• The instruction that can run only in kernel mode are called privileged instruction. So, option (a) in incorrect.
• Use of threads provides concurrency with in a process. The major disadvantage if the kernel is single threaded, a system call of one thread will block the whole process and CPU may be idle during blocking period. So, option (b) is correct.
• Process larger than main memory can be executed because of demand paging. The OS itself loads pages of a process in main memory as required. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Continuous file allocation leads to external fragmentation because whenever a file is deleted a hole is created. So, option (d) is correct.

Q50 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Suppose we run the Bellman-Ford algorithm on a weighted graph with n vertices and observe that some distances decrease in iteration n + 1. Which of the following is not a valid conclusion.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
There is definitely a negative cycle, so there are negative edge weights and distances will continue to decrease. However, a large positive weight may have cancelled out small negative weights so there need not be a negative distance after n + 1 iterations.

Q63 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the simple graph G given in figure given below:

Then, which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
1. Recall that the diameter is the maximum of all shortest path lengths between pairs of vertices.
Note that the shortest path length between D and F is 3, and all other pairs of non-adjacent vertices share a neighbor.
2. One possible solution is (A, F, E, C, D, B, A). This cycle and its reverse should constitute all possible solutions.
3. One possible 3-coloring is: {A, D, E} red; {B, F} green; C blue. Because there exists an oddlength cycle (e.g. (B, D, C)), no 2-coloring exists. Therefore the given coloring uses the least possible number of colors.
4. True. This follows from the fact that there exist vertices with odd degree; e.g. B.
